steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the best description of Sir Winston Day based on the text. Key signals: his face looks like it belongs on a bust or stamp (distinguished appearance), his forehead seems to bulge with grey matter (implying great intelligence), but it would be 'impertinent to inquire too closely into the actual achievements his half-century of public service had produced' — a sardonic way of saying his accomplishments are unclear or underwhelming. His memoirs offer only partial light on the 'enigma.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'appearance of a distinguished figure, but it is uncertain whether he has accomplished anything to earn distinction': Matches the text precisely. His looks suggest greatness; his actual record is an 'enigma.' ✓
  • Choice B — 'maintained a modest profile even though he has served the public capably for many years': The text implies the opposite: he appears grand, not modest, and his capability is exactly what is in question. ✗
  • Choice C — 'looks like a person worthy of respect, but his memoirs reveal that some of his actions were dishonorable': The text never mentions dishonorable actions. The memoirs are described as incomplete, not damning. ✗
  • Choice D — 'would be a celebrated public figure if his achievements did not have to be kept secret': The text hints that there may be no great achievements to hide — the irony is ambiguity, not secrecy shielding real accomplishments. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
